task_yaml.task:
I have a dataset of real World Bank indicators sitting at `/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/worldbank_data/worldbank_indicators_2000_2023.csv`. It covers six countries (China, United States, Japan, Germany, India, Brazil) from 2000 to 2023, with five indicators: GDP (current US$), GDP per capita (current US$), total population, urban population (% of total), and CO2 emissions per capita (tonnes).

Please write a data-driven comparative economic report based on THIS dataset (not your own prior knowledge — read and compute over the CSV). I want a real analysis, not a summary of what you already think you know about these countries.

The report should:
1. Load the CSV and work directly from its values.
2. Compare the countries across multiple indicators, and connect them — for example, how total GDP compares with GDP per capita and population; how per-capita CO2 emissions rank versus what you might expect from total economic size; and how urbanization has changed over time.
3. Call out the most interesting findings with concrete numbers, and for each number say which indicator and year it comes from.
4. Include at least three charts (PNG images or an interactive HTML chart) saved under `/tmp_workspace/results/`, and reference them from the report. The charts must reflect the actual data (correct trends, axes, and country labels).

Save the written report to `/tmp_workspace/results/economic_report.md`, with the chart files alongside it. If any value is missing in the dataset for a given country/year, note it rather than inventing a number.
